summary: DeVault praises ffmpeg as one of the most important achievements in free software history, highlighting its creator Fabrice Bellard's talent for building software that renders all competing solutions obsolete. He demonstrates ffmpeg's power through a personal anecdote of rigging up a low-latency remote gaming stream with a single command. The post argues that ffmpeg has effectively closed the book on multimedia processing, with virtually all multimedia companies depending on it. DeVault positions ffmpeg as a pillar of FOSS achievement that has touched nearly every person who has consumed digital media.
key_points: Fabrice Bellard has a pattern of building software so good it makes every alternative obsolete, and ffmpeg exemplifies this
ffmpeg's versatility is demonstrated by constructing a working remote game streaming setup from scratch with a single command and an hour of tinkering
ffmpeg was one of the first large-scale FOSS projects to completely eradicate proprietary software in its niche
The multimedia problem space is effectively solved: you're more likely to patch ffmpeg than build something new
ffmpeg underpins virtually all modern multimedia consumption, from TV to movies to podcasts to online video
money_quotes: Bellard has a knack for building high-quality software which solves a problem so well that every other solution becomes obsolete shortly thereafter, and ffmpeg is no exception.
ffmpeg is notable for being one of the first large-scale FOSS projects to completely eradicate proprietary software in its niche.
The book is now closed on multimedia: ffmpeg is the solution to almost all of your problems.
And if it's not, you're more likely to patch ffmpeg than to develop something new.
It has touched the lives of every reader, whether they know it or not.
It is one of the most well-executed and important software projects of all time.
With an hour of tinkering and reading man pages, I was able to come up with a single command which produced a working remote video game streaming setup from scratch thanks to ffmpeg.
key_insight: ffmpeg represents the ideal of free software: a project so comprehensive and well-executed that it completely displaced proprietary alternatives and became the universal foundation for all multimedia processing.
